Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

Mul-Apin 1

11* mulgišAPIN dEN.LIL₂ a-lik pa-ni MUL-MEŠ šu-ut dEN.LIL₂

(1) The Plough, Ellil, leader of the stars of Ellil;

22* mulUR.BAR.RA gišNINDA₂ ša₂ mulAPIN

(2) the Wolf, seed-funnel of the Plough;

33* mulŠU.GI dEN.ME.ŠAR₂.RA mulGAM₃ dgam-lu₄

(3) the Old Man, Enmešara; the Crook, Gamlu;

44* mulMAŠ.TAB.BA GAL.GAL.LA dLUGAL.IR₉.RA u₃ dMES.LAM.TA.E₃.A

(4) the Great Twins, Lugalira and Meslamtaea;

55* mulMAŠ.TAB.BA TUR.TUR dLAL₃ u₃ dNIN.EZEN×GUD

(5) the Small Twins, Alammuš and Nin-EZENxGUD;

66* mulAL.LUL šu-bat da-nu₃

(6) the Crab, Anu's dwelling;

77* mulUR.GU.LA dla—ta-ra-ak

(7) the Lion, La-tarak;

88* MUL ša₂ ina GABA mulUR.GU.LA GUBzu mulLUGAL

(8) the star that stands in the Lion's breast: the King;

99* MUL-MEŠ um-mu-lu-tu₄ ša₂ ina KUN mulUR.GU.LA

(9) the twinkling stars which stand in the Lion's tail; the date-spadix of the Frond: Zarpanitu;

1010GUBzu sis-sin-nu da-ru₆ dzar-pa-ni-tu₄
1111* mulŠU.PA dEN.LIL₂ ša₂ ši-mat KUR i-šim-mu

(11) The ŠU.PA-constellation, Enlil who fixes the fate of the land;

1212* MUL ša₂ ina IGI-šu₂ GUBzu mulḫe₂-gal₂-a-a-u₂ SUKKAL dNIN.LIL₂

(12) the star which stands in front of it: the Abundant, Ninlil's minister;

1313* MUL ša₂ EGIR-šu₂ GUBzu mulBAL.TEŠ₂.A SUKKAL dMUŠ₂

(13) the star which stands behind it: Dignity, Tišpak's minister;

1414* mulMAR.GID₂.DA dNIN.LIL₂

(14) the Wagon, Ninlil;

1515* MUL ša₂ KI za-ri-i ša₂ mulMAR.GID₂.DA GUBzu

(15) the star which stands in the Wagon's shaft: the Fox, Erra, strongest of the gods;

1616mulKA₅.A dER₃.RA gaš-ri DINGIR-MEŠ
1717* MUL ša₂ ina SAG.KI mulMAR.GID₂.DA GUBzu mulU₈ da-a

(17) the star which stands in front of the Wagon: the Ewe, Aya;

1818* mulMU.GID₂.KEŠ₂.DA da-nu₃ GALu₂ ša₂ ANe

(18) the Hitched Yoke: great Anu of heaven;

1919* mulMAR.GID₂.DA.AN.NA dDAM.KI.AN.NA

(19) the Wagon Of Heaven, Damkiana;

2020* MUL ša₂ ina ṭur-ri-šu₂ GUBzu mulIBILA—E₂.MAḪ

(20) the star which stands in its knot: the Heir of the Emah temple, the foremost son of Anu;

2121DUMU reš-tu-u₂ ša₂ da-nu-um
2222* mulDINGIR.GUB.BA-MEŠ šu-ut E₂.KUR mulDINGIR.TUŠ.A-MEŠ šu-ut E₂.KUR

(22) the Standing Gods of the Ekur temple; the Sitting Gods of the Ekur temple;

2323* mulUZ₃ dGU.LA

(23) the Goat: Gula;

2424* MUL ša₂ ina IGI mulUZ₃ GUBzu mulUR.GI₇

(24) the star which stands in front of the Goat: the Dog;

2525* MUL ne₂-bu-u₂ ša₂ mulUZ₃ dLAMMA SUKKAL dba-ba₆

(25) the bright star of the Goat: Lamassu, Baba's minister

2626* 2 MUL-MEŠ ša₂ EGIR-šu₂ GUB-MEŠzu dNIN.SAR u dER₃.RA.GAL

(26) the 2 stars which stand behind it: Ninsakar and Erragal;

2727* mulU₄.KA.DU₈.A dNERGALₓ(U.GUR)

(27) the Leopard: Nergal;

2828* MUL ša₂ ina ZA₃-šu₂ GUBzu mulŠAḪ dDA.MU

(28) the star which stands on its right: the Pig, Damu;

2929* MUL ša₂ ina GUB₃-šu₂ GUBzu mulANŠE.KUR.RA

(29) the star which stands on its left: the Horse;

3030* MUL ša₂ EGIR-šu₂ GUBzu mulLU.LIM SUKKAL MUL.MUL

(30) the star which stands behind it: the Stag, the Stars' minister;

3131* MUL-MEŠ um-mu-lu-tu₄ ša₂ ina GABA mulLU.LIM

(31) the twinkling stars which stand in the Stag's chest: the Vole, the Rainbow;

3232GUB-MEŠzu dḫar-ri-ru dTIR.AN.NA
3333* MUL SA₅ ne₂-bu-u₂ ša₂ ina ELLAG₂ mulLU.LIM GUBzu mulKA.MUŠ.I₃.GU₇.E

(33) the bright, red star which stands in the Stag's kidney: the Obliterator.

3434ki-ma MUL-MEŠ šu-ut dEN.LIL₂ ug-dam-mi-ru-ni

(34) When the stars of Ellil have been assembled, the great star, its light dim, divides heaven and stands (there): Marduk's star, the Ferry.

3535* MUL GAL U₄.DA-su da-aʾ-mat ANe BAR-ma GUBzu MUL dAMAR.UTU ne₂-bi-ri
3636* mulSAG.ME.GAR KI.GUB-su KUR₂.KUR₂ir ANe ib-bir

(36) Jupiter keeps changing its position (and) crosses heaven.

373733 MUL-MEŠ šu-ut dEN.LIL₂

(37) 33 stars of Ellil.

3838* mul1(IKU)gana₂ šu-bat de₂-a a-lik IGI MUL-MEŠ šu-ut da-nu₃

(38) The Field, Ea's dwelling, leader of the stars of Anu;

3939* MUL ša₂ ina IGIet mul1(IKU)gana₂ GUBzu mulši-nu-nu-tu₄

(39) the star which stands opposite the Field: the Swallow;

4040* MUL ša₂ EGIR mul1(IKU)gana₂ GUBzu mula-nu-ni-tu₄

(40) the star which stands behind the Field: Annunitu;

4141* MUL ša₂ EGIR-šu₂ GUBzu mulLU₂.ḪUN.GA₂ dDUMU.ZI

(41) the star which stands behind it: the Hireling, Dumuzi;

4242* MUL.MUL d7.BI DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ

(42) the Bristle: the Seven, the great gods;

4343* mulGU₄.AN.NA dis le-e AGA da-nu₃

(43) the Bull of Heaven; the Bull's jaw: Anu's crown;

4444* mulSIPA.ZI.AN.NA dPAP.SUKKAL SUKKAL da-nu₃ u INANA

(44) the True Shepherd of Anu: Papsukkal, minister of Anu and Ištar;

4545* mulMAŠ.TAB.BA ša₂ ina IGIet mulSIPA.ZI.AN.NA

(45) the Twins which stand in front of the True Shepherd of Anu: Lulal and La-tarak;

4646GUB-MEŠzu dLU₂.LAL₃ u dla—ta-ra-ak
4747* MUL ša₂ EGIR-šu₂ GUBzu mulDAR.LUGAL

(47) the star which stands behind it: the Rooster;

4848* mulKAK.SI.SA₂ šil-ta-ḫu UR.SAG GALu₂ dNIN.URTA

(48) the Arrow: the arrow, the great hero Ninurta;

4949* mulPAN diš-tar ELAM.MAtu₄ DUMU.MUNUS dEN.LIL₂

(49) the Bow: Ištar the Elamite, Ellil's daughter;

5050* muldMUŠ dNIN.GIŠ.ZI.DA EN er-ṣe-tu₄

(50) the Snake: Ningizzida, lord of the Underworld;

5151* mulUGAmušen a-re-bu MUL dIŠKUR

(51) the Raven: Adad's star;

5252* mulAB.SIN₂ dŠA.LA šu-bu-ul-tu₄

(52) the Furrow: Šala, the ear of grain;

5353* mulZI.BA.AN.NA SI mulGIR₂.TAB

(53) the Scales: the Scorpion's horn;

5454* muldza-ba₄-ba₄ mulTI₈mušen u mulAD₆

(54) Zababa; the Eagle; and the Corpse.

5555* muldele-bat KI.GUB-su KUR₂.KUR₂ir-ma ANe ib-bir

(55) Venus keeps changing its position and crosses heaven.

5656* mulṣal-bat-a-nu KI.GUB-su KUR₂.KUR₂ir-ma ANe ib-bir

(56) Mars keeps changing its position and crosses heaven.

5757* mulUDU.IDIM.SAG.UŠ KI.GUB-su KUR₂.KUR₂ir-ma ANe ib-bir

(57) Saturn keeps changing its position and crosses heaven.

5858* mulUDU.IDIM.GU₄.U₄ ša₂ dMAŠ MU-NI lu-u₂ ina dUTU.E₃.A

(58) Mercury, whose name is Ninurta, rises each month (and) sets each month, either in the east or in the west.

5959lu-u₂ ina dUTU.ŠU₂.A e-ma ITI IGI.LA₂-ma e-ma ITI TUM₃
606023 MUL-MEŠ šu-ut da-nu₃

(60) 23 stars of Anu.

6161* mulKU₆ de₂-a a-lik IGI MUL-MEŠ šu-ut de₂-a

(61) The Fish, the leader of the Stars of Ea;

6262* mulGU.LA de₂-a mulERIDUki de₂-a

(62) the Great One, Ea; Eridu, Ea;

6363* MUL ša₂ ina 15-šu₂ GUBzu mulNIN.MAḪ

(63) the star which stands on its right: Ninmah;

6464* mulEN.TE.NA.BAR.ḪUM dNIN.GIR₂.SU

(64) the Mouse; Ningirsu;

6565* MUL ša₂ ina A₂-šu₂ GUBzu mulgišGANA₂.UR₃ gišTUKUL ša₂ dA—E₂

(65) the star which stands at its side: the Harrow, Mar-biti's weapon, inside which one sees the Apsu;

6666ša₂ ina lib₃-bi-šu₂ ABZU i-bar-ru-u₂
6767* 2 MUL-MEŠ ša₂ EGIR-šu₂ GUB-MEzu dŠULLAT u dḪANIŠ dUTU u dIŠKUR

(67) the 2 stars which stand behind it: Šullat and Haniš, (or) Šamaš and Adad;

6868* MUL ša₂ EGIR-šu₂-nu GUBzu GIM de₂-a KURḫa

(68) the star which stands behind them, rises like Ea (and) sets like Ea: Numušda, Adad;

6969GIM de₂-a ŠU₂-MEŠ mulNU.MUŠ.DA dIŠKUR
7070* MUL ša₂ ina GUB₃ mulGIR₂.TAB GUBzu mulUR.IDIM dKU₃.SU₃

(70) the star which stands on the Scorpion's left: Wild Dog, Kusu;

7171* mulGIR₂.TAB diš-ḫa-ra be-let da-ad₂-me

(71) the Scorpion: Ishara, lady of the inhabited world;

7272* mulGABA.GIR₂.TAB dLI₉.SI₄ dNA₃

(72) the Scorpion's Chest: Lisi, Nabu;

7373* 2 MUL-MEŠ ša₂ ina zi-qit mulGIR₂.TAB GUB-MEŠzu

(73) the 2 stars which stand in the Scorpion's sting: Šarur and Šargaz;

7474dŠAR₂.UR₄ u dŠAR₂.GAZ
7575* MUL ša₂ EGIR-šu₂-nu GUBzu mulPA.BIL.SAG

(75) the star that stands behind them: Pabilsag;

7676* mulMA₂.GUR₈ u mulSUḪUR.MAŠ₂ku₆

(76) the Barge; and the Goatfish.

777715 MUL-MEŠ šu-ut de₂-a

(77) 15 stars of Ea.

7878* ina itiBARA₂ U₄ 1-KAM mulLU₂.ḪUN.GA₂ IGI.LA₂

(78) In Nisannu (I), on the 1st day, the Hireling appears.

7979* ina itiBARA₂ U₄ 20-KAM mulGAM₃ IGI.LA₂

(79) In Nisannu (I), on the 20th day, the Crook appears.

8080* ina itiGU₄ U₄ 1-KAM MUL.MUL IGI.LA₂

(80) In Ayyaru (II), on the 1st day, the Bristle appears.

8181* ina itiGU₄ U₄ 20-KAM mulis le-e IGI.LA₂

(81) In Ayyaru (II), on the 20th day, the Bull's Jaw appears.

8282* ina itiSIG₄ U₄ 10-KAM mulSIPA.ZI.AN.NA u mulMAŠ.TAB.BA GAL.GAL IGI-ME

(82) In Simanu (III), on the 10th day, the True Shepherd of Anu and the Great Twins appear.

8383* ina itiŠU U₄ 5-KAM mulMAŠ.TAB.BA TUR.TUR u mulAL.LUL IGI-ME

(83) In Du'uzu (IV), on the 5th day, the Small Twins and the Crab appear.

8484* ina itiŠU U₄ 15-KAM mulKAK.SI.SA₂ mulMUŠ u mulUR.GU.LA

(84) In Du'uzu (IV), on the 15th day, the Arrow, the Snake, and the Lion appear and the daytime watch is 4 minas, the night-time watch 2 minas.

8585IGI-ME-ma 4 MA.NA EN.NUN u₄-me 2 MA.NA EN.NUN GE₆
8686* ina itiNE U₄ 5-KAM mulPAN u mulLUGAL IGI-ME

(86) In Abu (V), on the 5th day, the Bow and the King appear.

8787* ina itiKIN U₄ 1-KAM mul[...]

(87) In Ululu (VI), on the 1st day, [...].

8888* ina itiKIN U₄ 10-KAM mulERIDUki u mulUGAmušen IGI-ME

(88) In Ululu (VI), on the 10th day, Eridu and the Raven appear.

8989* ina itiKIN U₄ 15-KAM mulŠU.PA dEN.LIL₂ IGI

(89) In Ululu (VI), on the 15th day, the ŠU.PA-constellation, Enlil appears.

9090* ina itiKIN U₄ 25-KAM mulAB.SIN₂ IGI

(90) In Ululu (VI), on the 25th day, the Furrow appears.

9191* ina itiDU₆ U₄ 15-KAM mulzi-ba-ni-tu₄ mulUR.IDIM mulEN.TE.NA.BAR.ḪUM

(91) In Tašritu (VII), on the 15th day, the Scales, the Wild Dog, the Mouse, and the Dog apear, and the daytime watch is 3 minas, the night-time watch is 3 minas.

9292u mulUR.GI₇ IGI-ME-ma 3 MA.NA EN.NUN u₄-mi 3 MA.NA EN.NUN GE₆
9393* ina itiAPIN U₄ 5-KAM mulGIR₂.TAB IGI

(93) In Arahsamnu (VIII), on the 5th day, the Scorpion appears.

9494* ina itiAPIN U₄ 15-KAM mulUZ₃ u mulGABA.GIR₂.TAB IGI-ME

(94) In Arahsamnu (VIII), on the 15th day, the Goat and the Scorpion's Chest appear.

9595* ina itiGAN U₄ 15-KAM mulU₄.KA.DU₈.A mulTI₈mušen u mulPA.BIL.SAG IGI-MEŠ

(95) In Kislimu (IX), on the 15th day, the Leopard, the Eagle, and Pabilsag appear.

9696* ina itiAB U₄ 15-KAM mulSIM.MAḪ mulši-nu-nu-tu₄ mulIM.ŠEŠ

(96) In Ṭebetu (X), on the 15th day, the Swallow—the Swallow, the IM.ŠEŠ-star—appears at sunrise and the Arrow appears in the evening, and the daytime watch is 2 minas, the night-time watch 4 minas.

9797ina GIŠ.NIM IGI.LA₂ u mulKAK.SI.SA₂ ina li-la-a-ti
9898IGI.LA₂-ma 2 MA.NA EN.NUN u₄-me 4 MA.NA EN.NUN GE₆
9999* ina itiZIZ₂ U₄ 5-KAM mulGU.LA mul1(IKU)gana₂ u mulLU.LIM IGI-MEŠ

(99) In Šabaṭu (XI), on the 5th day, the Great One, the Field, and the Stag appear.

100100* ina itiZIZ₂ U₄ 25-KAM mula-nu-ni-tu₄ IGI.LA₂

(100) In Šabaṭu (XI), on the 25th day, Annunitu appears.

101101* ina itiŠE U₄ 15-KAM mulKU₆ u mulŠU.GI IGI.LA₂-ME

(101) In Addaru (XII), on the 15th day, the Fish and the Old Man appear.

102102* MUL.MUL KUR-ma mulGIR₂.TAB ŠU₂bi

(102) The Bristle rises and the Scorpion sets.

103103* mulGIR₂.TAB KUR-ma MUL.MUL ŠU₂bi

(103) The Scorpion rises and the Bristle sets.

104104* mulGU₄.AN.NA KUR-ma mulŠU.PA ŠU₂bi

(104) The Bull of Heaven rises and the ŠU.PA-constellation sets.

105105* mulSIPA.ZI.AN.NA KUR-ma mulPA.BIL.SAG ŠU₂bi

(105) The True Shepherd of Anu rises and Pabilsag sets.

106106* mulKAK.SI.SA₂ muldMUŠ u mulUR.GU.LA KUR-MEnim-ma

(106) The Arrow, the Snake, and the Lion rise and the Great One and the Eagle set.

107107mulGU.LA u mulTI₈mušen ŠU₂-MEŠ
108108* mulPAN u mulLUGAL KUR-ME-ma mulUZ₃ ŠU₂bi

(108) The Bow and the King rise and the Goat sets.

109109* mulERIDUki u mulUGAmušen KUR-MEŠ-ma mulU₄.KA.DU₈.A ŠU₂bi

(109) Eridu and the Raven rise and the Leopard sets.

110110* mulŠU.PA dEN.LIL₂ KUR-ma mul1(IKU)gana₂ ŠU₂bi

(110) The the ŠU.PA-constellation, Enlil, rises and the Field sets.

111111* mulNIN.MAḪ KUR-ma mula-nu-ni-tu₄ ŠU₂bi

(111) Ninmah rises and Annunitu sets.

112112* mulzi-ba-ni-tu₄ mulUR.IDIM u mulEN.TE.NA.BAR.ḪUM

(112) The Scales, the Wild Dog, and the Mouse rise and the Hireling sets.

113113KUR-ME-ma mulLU₂.ḪUN.GA₂ ŠU₂bi
114114* mulGIR₂.TAB u mulUR.GI₇ KUR-ME-ma mulERIDUki u MUL.MUL ŠU₂-MEŠ

(114) The Scorpion and the Dog rise, and Eridu and the Bristle set.

115115* mulGABA.GIR₂.TAB u mulUZ₃ KUR-MEŠ-ma mulŠU.GI u mulSIPA.ZI.AN.NA ŠU₂-MEŠ

(115) The Scorpion's Chest and the Goat rise, and the Old Man and the True Shepherd of Anu set.

116116* mulPA.BIL.SAG mulza-ba₄-ba₄ u mulDINGIR.GUB.BA-MEŠ KUR-MEŠ-ma

(116) Pabilsag, Zababa, and the Standing Gods rise and the Arrow, the Bow, and the Crook set.

117117mulKAK.SI.SA₂ mulPAN u mulGAM₃ ŠU₂-MEŠ
118118* mulU₄.KA.DU₈.A u mulTI₈mušen KUR-MEŠ-ma

(118) The Leopard and the Eagle rise, and the Great Twins and the Small Twins set.

119119mulMAŠ.TAB.BA GAL.GAL u mulMAŠ.TAB.BA TUR.TUR ŠU₂-MEŠ
120120* mul1(IKU)gana₂ mulGU.LA u mulLU.LIM KUR-MEŠ-ma

(120) The Field, the Great One, and the Stag rise, and the Lion, the Snake, and the Mouse set.

121121mulUR.GU.LA muldMUŠ u mulEN.TE.NA.BAR.ḪUM ŠU₂-MEŠ
122122* mulKU₆ u mulŠU.GI KUR-MEŠ-ma mulAB.SIN₂ u mulUR.IDIM ŠU₂-MEŠ

(122) The Fish and the Old Man rise, and the Furrow and the Wild Dog set.

123123* TA KUR ša₂ mulKAK.SI.SA₂ 55 U₄-MEŠ ana KUR ša₂ mulERIDUki

(123) From the rising of the Arrow it is 55 days to the rising of Eridu.

124124* TA KUR ša₂ mulKAK.SI.SA₂ 1 ŠU U₄-MEŠ ana KUR ša₂ mulŠU.PA

(124) From the rising of the Arrow1 it is 1 sixty days to the rising of the ŠU.PA-constellation.

125125* TA KUR ša₂ mulŠU.PA 10 U₄-MEŠ ana KUR ša₂ mulAB.SIN₂

(125) From the rising of the ŠU.PA-constellation it is 10 days to the rising of the Furrow.

126126* TA KUR ša₂ mulAB.SIN₂ 20 U₄-MEŠ ana KUR ša₂ mulzi-ba-ni-tu₄

(126) From the rising of the Furrow it is 20 days to the rising of the Scales.

127127* TA KUR ša₂ mulzi-ba-ni-tu₄ 30 U₄-MEŠ ana KUR ša₂ mulUZ₃

(127) From the rising of the Scales it is 30 days to the rising of the Goat.

128128* TA KUR ša₂ mulUZ₃ 30 U₄-MEŠ ana KUR ša₂ mulU₄.KA.DU₈.A

(128) From the rising of the Goat it is 30 days to the rising of the Leopard.

129129* TA KUR ša₂ mulU₄.KA.DU₈.A 30 U₄-MEŠ ana KUR ša₂ mulSIM.MAḪ

(129) From the rising the Leopard it is 30 days to the rising of the Swallow.

130130* TA KUR ša₂ mulSIM.MAḪ 20 U₄-MEŠ ana KUR ša₂ mul1(IKU)gana₂

(130) From the rising of the Swallow it is 20 days to the rising of the Field.

131131* TA KUR ša₂ mul1(IKU)gana₂ 40 U₄-MEŠ ana KUR ša₂ mulKU₆

(131) From the rising of the Field it is 40 days to the rising of the Fish.

132132* TA KUR ša₂ mulKU₆ 35 U₄-MEŠ ana KUR ša₂ mulGAM₃

(132) From the rising of the Fish it is 35 days to the rising of the Crook.

133133* TA KUR ša₂ mulGAM₃ 10 U₄-MEŠ ana KUR ša₂ MUL.MUL

(133) From the rising of the Crook it is 10 days to the rising of the Bristle.

134134* TA KUR ša₂ MUL.MUL 20 U₄-MEŠ ana KUR ša₂ mulGU₄.AN.NA

(134) From the rising of the Bristle it is 20 days to the rising of the Bull of Heaven.

135135* TA KUR ša₂ mulGU₄.AN.NA 20 U₄-MEŠ ana KUR ša₂ mulSIPA.ZI.AN.NA

(135) From the rising of the Bull of Heaven it is 20 days to the rising of the True Shepherd of Anu.

136136* TA KUR ša₂ mulSIPA.ZI.AN.NA 35 U₄-MEŠ ana KUR ša₂ mulKAK.SI.SA₂

(136) From the rising of the True Shepherd of Anu it is 35 days to the rising of the Arrow.

137137* TA KUR ša₂ mulKAK.SI.SA₂ 20 U₄-MEŠ ana KUR ša₂ mulPAN

(137) From the rising of the Arrow it is 20 days to the rising of the Bow.

138138u₄-mu 1 UŠ-TA.AM₃ MUL-MEŠ ina šer₃-ti ana GE₆ KU₄-MEŠni

(138) The stars set by 1 UŠ a day from morning to night.

139139u₄-mu 1 UŠ-TA.AM₃ MUL-MEŠ ina li-la-a-ti ana u₄-me E₃-MEŠni

(139) The stars rise by 1 UŠ a day from evening to day.

140140* MUL-MEŠ ša₂ ziq-pi ša₂ ina KASKAL šu-ut dEN.LIL₂ ina MURU₂ ANe

(140) The culminating stars that stand in the path of Ellil in the middle of heaven, opposite the sky-watcher's chest, and by means of which he observes the risings and settings of the stars at night:

141141ina IGIet GABA ša₂ URU₃ ANe GUB-MEŠ-ma GE₆ KUR u ŠU₂bi
142142ša₂ MUL-MEŠ ina lib₃-bi-šu₂-nu im-ma-ru
143143* mulŠU.PA mulBAL.TEŠ₂.A mulDINGIR.GUB.BA-MEŠ mulUR.GI₇

(143) The ŠU.PA-constellation, Dignity, the Standing Gods, the Dog, the Goat, the Leopard, the Stag, the Old Man, the Crook, the Great Twins, the Crab, the Lion, the Frond, and the Abundant.

144144mulUZ₃ mulU₄.KA.DU₈.A mulLU.LIM mulŠU.GI mulGAM₃
145145mulMAŠ.TAB.BA GAL.GAL mulAL.LUL mulUR.GU.LA mula-ru₆ u mulḫe₂-gal₂-la-a-a
146146PAP an-nu-tu MUL-MEŠ ša₂ ziq-pi ša₂ KASKAL šu-ut dEN.LIL₂ ša₂ ina MURU₂ ANe

(146) All these are the culminating stars that stand in the path of Ellil in the middle of heaven, opposite your chest, and by means of which you observe the risings and settings of the stars at night.

147147ina IGIet GABA-ka GUB-MEzu-ma GE₆ MU₂-MEŠ u ŠU₂-MEŠ
148148ša₂ MUL-MEŠ ina lib₃-bi-šu₂-nu tam-ma-ru
149149šum₄-ma zi-iq-pa a-na a-ma-ri-ka ina itiBARA₂ U₄ 20-KAM

(149) If you are to observe the culmination, in Nisannu (I) on the 20th day you stand with west to your right, east to your left, your face raised south, in the morning before the Sun has risen.

150150ina šer₃-ti la-am dUTU KURḫa GUB-ma ZA₃-ka tu₁₅MAR.TU
151151GUB₃-ka tu₁₅KUR.RA ni-iš IGI-ka tu₁₅U₁₈.LU
152152qu₂-ma-ru ša₂ mulU₄.KA.DU₈.A ina MURU₂ ANe

(152) The Leopard's shoulder stands in the middle of heaven opposite your chest, and the Crook rises.

153153ina IGIet GABA-ka GUB-ma mulGAM₃ KURḫa
154154* ina itiGU₄ U₄ 1-KAM GABA ša₂ mulU₄.KA.DU₈.A ina MURU₂ ANe

(154) In Ayyaru (II), on the 1st day, the Leopard's chest stands in the middle of heaven opposite your chest, and the Bristle rises.

155155IGIet GABA-ka GUB-ma MUL.MUL KURḫa
156156* ina itiGU₄ U₄ 20-KAM ki-in-ṣu ša₂ mulU₄.KA.DU₈.A

(156) In Ayyaru (II), on the 20th day, the Leopard's shin stands in the middle of heaven opposite your chest, and the Bull's Jaw rises.

157157ina MURU₂ ANe IGIet GABA-ka GUB-ma mulis le-e KURḫa
158158* ina itiSIG₄ U₄ 10-KAM a-si-du ša₂ mulU₄.KA.DU₈.A ina MURU₂ ANe

(158) In Simanu (III), on the 10th day, the Leopard's heel stands in the middle of heaven opposite your chest, and the True Shepherd of Anu rises.

159159IGIet GABA-ka GUB-ma mulSIPA.ZI.AN.NA KURḫa
160160* ina itiŠU U₄ 15-KAM MUL ne₂-bu-u₂ ša₂ mulŠU.GI ina MURU₂ ANe

(160) In Du'uzu (IV), on the 15th day, the brightest star of the Old Man stands in the middle of heaven opposite your chest, and the Arrow rises.

161161IGIet GABA-ka GUB-ma mulKAK.SI.SA₂ KURḫa
162162* ina itiNE U₄ 15-KAM MUL-MEŠ um-mu-lu-tu₄ ša₂ mulŠU.GI

(162) In Abu (V), on the 15th day, the twinkling stars of the Old Man stand in the middle of heaven opposite your chest, and the Bow rises.

163163ina MURU₂ ANe IGIet GABA-ka GUB-ma mulPAN KURḫa
164164* ina itiKIN U₄ 15-KAM mulMAŠ.TAB.BA GAL.GAL ina MURU₂ ANe

(164) In Ululu (VI), on the 15th day, the Great Twins stand in the middle of heaven opposite your chest, and the ŠU.PA-constellation and Eridu rise.

165165IGIet GABA-ka GUB-ma mulŠU.PA u mulERIDUki KUR-MEŠni
166166* ina itiDU₆ U₄ 15-KAM mulUR.GU.LA ina MURU₂ ANe

(166) In Tašritu (VII), on the 15th day, the Lion stands in the middle of heaven opposite your chest, and the Scales rise.

167167IGIet GABA-ka GUB-ma mulzi-ba-ni-tu₄ KURḫa
168168* ina itiAPIN U₄ 15-KAM mula-ru₆ ina MURU₂ ANe

(168) In Arahsamnu (VIII), on the 15th day, the Frond stands in the middle of heaven opposite your chest, and the Goat rises.

169169IGIet GABA-ka GUB-ma mulUZ₃ KURḫa
170170* ina itiGAN U₄ 15-KAM mulŠU.PA MIN<(ina)> MURU₂ ANe MIN<(IGIet)> GABA-ka GUB-ma mulU₄.KA.DU₈.A KURḫa

(170) In Kislimu (IX), on the 15th day, the ŠU.PA-constellation ditto, ditto, the Leopard rises.

171171* ina itiAB U₄ 15-KAM mulDINGIR.GUB.BA-MEŠ MIN<(ina)> MURU₂ ANe IGIet GABA-ka GUB-MEŠ mulSIM.MAḪ KURḫa

(171) In Ṭebetu (X), on the 15th day, the Standing Gods stand ditto opposite your chest, and the Swallow rises.

172172* ina itiZIZ₂ U₄ 15-KAM mulUR.GI₇ MIN<(ina)> MURU₂ ANe IGIet GABA-ka GUB-ma mul1(IKU)gana₂ KURḫa

(172) In Šabaṭu (XI), on the 15th day, the Dog stands ditto opposite your chest, and the Field rises.

173173* ina itiŠE U₄ 15-KAM mulUZ₃ MIN<(ina)> MURU₂ ANe IGIet GABA-ka GUB-ma mulKU₆ KURḫa

(173) In Addaru (XII), on the 15th day, the Goat stands ditto opposite your chest, and the Fish rises.

174174DINGIR-MEŠ ša₂ i-na KASKAL d30 GUB-MEŠ-ma d30 e-ma ITI

(174) The gods that stand in the Moon's path, and across which the Moon traverses and touches each month:

175175ina pi-rik-šu₂-nu DIB-MEŠ-ma TAG-MEŠ-šu₂-nu-ti
176176MUL.MUL mulGU₄.AN.NA mulSIPA.ZI.AN.NA mulŠU.GI

(176) The Bristle, the Bull of Heaven, the True Shepherd of Anu, the Old Man, the Crook, the Great Twins, the Crab, the Lion, the Furrow, the Scales, the Scorpion, Pabilsag, the Goatfish, the Great One, the Swallow's Tails, Annunitu, and the Hireling.

177177mulGAM₃ mulMAŠ.TAB.BA GAL.GAL mulAL.LUL mulUR.GU.LA
178178mulAB.SIN₂ mulzi-ba-ni-tu₄ mulGIR₂.TAB mulPA.BIL.SAG
179179mulSUḪUR.MAŠ₂ mulGU.LA KUN-MEŠ mulSIM.MAḪ
180180mula-nu-ni-tu₄ u mulLU₂.ḪUN.GA₂
181181PAP an-nu-tu₄ DINGIR-MEŠ ša₂ ina KASKAL d30 GUB-MEŠ-ma d30 e-ma ITI

(181) All these are the gods that stand in the Moon's path, and across which the Moon traverses and touches each month.

182182[ina pi-rik-šu₂-nu DIB]-MEŠ-ma TAG-MEŠ-šu₂-nu-ti

1 I expect "Eridu " here.
